title: gratiola peruviana
intro: Gratiola peruviana, also known as Lojane or Peruvian bugle, is a perennial plant species native to the mountainous regions of South America. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its medicinal properties of antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant actions. The health benefits of Gratiola peruviana include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce anxiety and stress, and act as an antiseptic to prevent infections. Gratiola peruviana can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture using its leaves and flowers, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions.
